name: Proton lifetime exceeds Super-Kamiokande limits
requirement: Any candidate framework that predicts proton decay must do so with a partial lifetime exceeding the Super-Kamiokande limits. The strongest single-channel constraint is on p -> e+ pi^0, with tau/B > 2.4 x 10^34 years at 90% CL. The p -> nu_bar K^+ channel (favored by SUSY GUTs) is constrained to tau/B > 5.9 x 10^33 years.

regime.description: Proton-scale lengths and the proton mass itself; baryon-number-violating decays.

current_bound.value: tau/B(p -> e+ pi^0) > 2.4e34 years; tau/B(p -> nu_bar K+) > 5.9e33 years
current_bound.reference: Super-K Collaboration, 'Search for proton decay via p -> e+ pi^0 and p -> mu+ pi^0 in 0.31 megaton-years exposure of the Super-Kamiokande water Cherenkov detector', PRD 102, 112011 (2020), arXiv:2010.16098

what_it_excludes: Most minimal SU(5) Grand Unified Theories were already excluded by earlier IMB and Kamiokande limits; the current Super-K bounds also exclude minimal SO(10) GUTs and place severe constraints on supersymmetric SU(5) and SO(10) variants. Hyper-Kamiokande (operational ~2027) will probe an order of magnitude further, potentially closing minimal SUSY GUTs entirely.

parameterization.name: GUT-style: tau(p -> e+ pi^0) ~ M_GUT^4 / (alpha_GUT^2 m_p^5), with model-dependent O(1) coefficients. More generally, dimension-six baryon-number-violating SMEFT operators with Wilson coefficients C_BNV / Lambda^2.
parameterization.reference: Particle Data Group, Review of Particle Physics, 'Grand Unified Theories' chapter; Nath and Fileviez Perez, Phys. Rep. 441, 191 (2007), arXiv:hep-ph/0601023.
parameterization.summary: In GUTs, p decay is mediated by exchange of heavy gauge bosons of mass M_GUT, with rate scaling as 1/M_GUT^4. SMEFT contains four BNV dim-6 operators (O_duue, O_qque, etc.) whose Wilson coefficients are bounded by current limits.

procedural.predicted_observable: tau_p^-1 = sum_channels Gamma(p -> X) where each channel has a calculable matrix element times C_BNV^2 / Lambda^4.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: M_GUT > 10^16 GeV in minimal SU(5) (already excluded). For SUSY GUTs: M_GUT >~ 10^15-16 GeV survives; tighter constraints from p -> nu_bar K+ channel. SMEFT BNV Wilson coefficients: |C_BNV / Lambda^2| < 10^-31 GeV^-2.
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts proton decay, compute the partial widths into the major channels (e+ pi^0, mu+ pi^0, nu_bar K+, e+ K^0, etc.) using the framework's effective BNV operators.
Sum partial widths to total decay rate; require lifetime exceeds Super-K bounds in every channel.
procedural.notes: Nucleon matrix elements relevant for proton decay are now known from lattice QCD to ~10% precision (e.g. RBC/UKQCD), so the matching from BNV Wilson coefficients to lifetimes is a controlled calculation.

primary_references.citation: Super-Kamiokande Collaboration, 'Search for proton decay via p -> e+ pi^0 and p -> mu+ pi^0', PRD 102, 112011 (2020)
Super-Kamiokande Collaboration, 'Search for proton decay via p -> nu_bar K+ using 260 kiloton-year data of Super-Kamiokande', PRD 90, 072005 (2014)
